prompt:
Create a black-line-art medical instruction illustration in the universal patient-information-leaflet style: uniform medium-weight black ink lines on pure white background, minimal short parallel hatching for soft shadow only, no color except red blood inside the vial, no frame, no border, no captions, no labels, no logos, no numbers.

=== CRITICAL ANCHOR ===
The FIRST provided reference image (protector tube top.png — the real product photo of the assembled protector tube laid on its side) IS THE EXACT MODEL for the protector tube's appearance: its SHAPE, its PROPORTIONS, and its TEXT ORIENTATION. The protector tube drawn in your output MUST be a faithful line-art replica of THIS PHOTO when stood up vertically with cap on top.

STUDY THE PHOTO REFERENCE:
  • The assembled tube is STUBBY and CHUNKY — total length is only about 1.3x its diameter. WIDER than it is tall when laid flat. Like a fat hockey-puck stack, NOT a tall test tube.
  • The BLACK CAP is a short squat solid-black cylinder. Cap length (along the tube axis) is about 0.5x its diameter — very short. The cap is wider than it is long.
  • The WHITE BODY is a slightly taller cylinder of the SAME WIDE DIAMETER as the cap. Body length is about 0.9–1.0x its diameter.
  • The cap and body share the same wide diameter. When assembled they form one continuous wide stout cylinder.
  • Text on the cap reads 'DIALED' embossed along the cap's length axis, with the FIRST D positioned at the CAP-END (the outer end of the cap, which becomes the TOP when the tube is stood upright with cap-on-top) and the last D at the body-junction end of the cap.
  • Text on the body reads 'HEALTH' embossed along the body's length axis, with the FIRST H positioned at the CAP-JUNCTION end (which is the TOP of the body when the tube is upright) and the last H at the body-base end.
  • Each letter is ROTATED 90° from upright — the letters lie ALONG the tube's length axis like text on a book spine. The viewer reads the text by tilting their head 90° (or imagining the embossing running along the cylinder's length).

=== COMPOSITION ===
A 1:1 square DUAL-STATE composition arranged side-by-side.

LEFT STATE (left half of frame): the upright WHITE PROTECTOR TUBE BODY by itself (no cap yet), open at its top, drawn as the FAITHFUL LINE-ART REPLICA of the body shown in the photo — same stubby cylinder, same wide diameter, same proportions (body length ≈ 1.0x diameter). 'HEALTH' embossed along the body's length axis, with the FIRST H at the TOP of the body (just below the open mouth) and the LAST H at the body's BASE. Each letter is a thin black outlined sans-serif capital, ROTATED 90° to lie along the tube's vertical axis (letter tops face one side of the tube, letter bottoms face the other side). Reading the body top-down (with head tilted 90°): H-E-A-L-T-H forward. A hand from upper-left holds a tall narrow capped blood-filled glass vial and lowers it DOWN into the body's open mouth.

MIDDLE: a SOLID BLACK CURVED ARROW pointing right, arching across the center.

RIGHT STATE (right half of frame): the ASSEMBLED upright protector tube — same WHITE BODY as in the left state (same stubby proportions, same HEALTH text in the same orientation, first H at top), with the BLACK CAP placed on top. The black cap is a short squat solid-black cylinder matching the photo's cap proportions (cap length ≈ 0.5x cap diameter, same wide diameter as the body). 'DIALED' embossed along the cap's length axis, with the FIRST D at the TOP of the cap (the cap-end, where the hand pinches it) and the LAST D at the bottom of the cap (just above the cap/body seam). Each letter is a thin WHITE outlined sans-serif capital against the solid-black cap (line-art embossed look), ROTATED 90° to lie along the cap's vertical axis. Reading the cap top-down (with head tilted 90°): D-I-A-L-E-D forward. A hand from upper-right pinches the cap and lowers it onto the body. Visible inside the body (through the open mouth, just below the cap): a faint glimpse of red blood from the seated vial.

Combined reading top-to-bottom of the assembled tube in the RIGHT state: BLACK CAP showing 'DIALED' forward (D-I-A-L-E-D) → WHITE BODY showing 'HEALTH' forward (H-E-A-L-T-H) = 'DIALED HEALTH' linearly top-down.

=== STRICT VISUAL ANCHOR ===
The protector tube's stubby SHAPE, its WIDE DIAMETER vs short height, and its TEXT ORIENTATION (letters running along the tube's length axis with first letter at the top of each section) must match the FIRST reference image (the product photo) EXACTLY. If the tube in your output is rendered tall and narrow like a test tube, that is WRONG. If the text on the tube reads BACKWARDS top-down (last letter at the top), that is WRONG. Both must replicate the product photo.

The SECOND reference image (v10 of step 21) is a STRUCTURE REFERENCE for the COMPOSITION ONLY — the dual-state layout, the hand poses, the vial, the arrow placement, the line-art style. But the TUBE SHAPE and TEXT in v10 are WRONG — IGNORE the tube in v10 and replace it with a faithful line-art replica of the product photo's tube.

No frame, no border, no captions, no labels, no logos, no numbers.
refImages: DIaled Health Instruction Graphics/Product Refs/protector tube top.png
